🟡 ReVillage Green Valley

Case Study
Y

network_affiliation
[[BioFi Project]]
background_section
## Background The Green Valley watershed is a compact [40-square-mile bioregion](https://youtu.be/nowoYmBRijw) in western Sonoma County, California, centered on Graton ([about 1,800 residents](https://youtu.be/nowoYmBRijw)). Rolling hills, vineyards, farmland, and forests define the area, with the watershed itself as a natural frame. Over the past century the local economy moved from apples toward wine; the community is now writing another chapter. ### Origins and Network ReVillage Green Valley grew out of grassroots organizing. They describe themselves as accidental bioregional organizers—the work began with love of place and gathering alongside neighbors who had long stewarded the land. They envision a local economy grounded in ecology, equity, and cultural renewal, co-created with First Nations peoples, Latino farmworkers, Zen communities, and other long-term residents. Wildfires, housing pressure, ecological decline, and isolation are the overlapping crises; their response is to reweave community through land-based regeneration and shared care.
Sources
33314415-10e1-81c5-b678-d3bda0742e07
patterns

temp
improper location but good for now?
description
ReVillage Green Valley is a place-rooted initiative reweaving community through land-based regeneration and collective care, responding to wildfires, housing displacement, ecological degradation, and social isolation.
vision_legacy_section
## Vision and Legacy They are watching how commons, shared culture, and a regenerative economy might braid over time, and welcome partners who want to prototype village-scale regeneration built for interdependence, cultural vitality, and ecological repair. By strengthening intergenerational, locally governed systems, they aim to offer a replicable model of “revillaging” for rural and peri-urban landscapes. The same crises—fire, displacement, ecological harm, isolation—are addressed by reweaving community through land-based regeneration and collective care. In the Green Valley watershed—hills, vineyards, farmland, forest—people are practicing interdependence with one another, with nonhuman nature, and with the living Earth: one gas station, one firehouse, one enterprise at a time, starting from love of place and neighbors who show up to care.
website
https://revillage.net/
bioregional_context
Green Valley exists within West Sonoma County, California, centered around the town of Graton with approximately 1,800 residents. The bioregion is defined by rolling hills, vineyards, farmland, and forests, with the Green Valley watershed serving as a natural boundary.
category
Bioregional Organizer
